title: Walking Through Color
author: Nick Cottrell
description: An essay about orthogonal channel architectures in biological sensory systems, engineered communication systems, and semantic coordinate spaces. Written on a walk with a Standard Poodle named Jack Jack.

sections.title: Opening
sections.subtitle: The walk, Jack Jack, the setup
sections.notes: Establishes the walk, the dog, the research question. Signal processing vocabulary (multiplexes, resolves) foreshadows WDM section. Thesis stated plainly: pattern matching vs actually seeing something.

sections.title: Part One: The Brain's RGB
sections.subtitle: Fudan EEG study, trichromatic sequencing
sections.notes: First concrete anchor. 93.7% decoding accuracy, sequential timing (R:190ms G:215ms B:238ms). Introduces VRGB as consequence of biology, not invention. The line 'I was being inevitable' is the spine.

sections.title: Part Two: The Dog's Computation
sections.subtitle: Nasal fluid dynamics, 811 receptor genes, combinatorial space
sections.notes: Flagged extrapolation zone ('red threads on the wall'). 811 receptor genes as combinatorial not parallel. The fire hydrant as tamper-proof chain. Jack Jack running a query.

sections.title: Part Three: The Color You Can't See
sections.subtitle: Hummingbird tetrachromacy, Princeton field study
sections.notes: Emotional pivot -- 'first I need to make you feel something.' 30% of plumage color is invisible to trichromats. Tetrahedron vs triangle geometry. Incommensurable perceptual worlds.

sections.title: Part Four: What the Engineers Found
sections.subtitle: Visible light communication, wavelength division multiplexing
sections.notes: The independent-discovery argument. Three orthogonal wavelengths in fiber optics = same architecture as retina/nose/eye. 'The principle is the same. The substrate is different.' This is where the essay earns its central claim.

sections.title: Part Five: The Mantis Shrimp Problem
sections.subtitle: Parallel lookup vs continuous space tradeoff
sections.notes: The counterargument section. 16 photoreceptors but not richer color -- categorical speed vs continuous nuance. Introduces the Hofstadter unit. This is the speculative peak -- earned by four sections of concrete evidence.

sections.title: Part Six: What I Think I'm Building
sections.subtitle: VRGB as native-dimensionality coordinate system, compression steering
sections.notes: The practical claim. 16.7M positions, Euclidean distance as semantic measure, compression steering for quantized models. Honest about uncertainty ('I'm still looking at the red threads'). References A Bridge in the Sky as test case.

sections.title: Coda
sections.subtitle: Jack Jack at the door
sections.notes: Returns to sodium orange, returns to the dog. Resolves pattern-matching vs seeing-something tension from opening. 'We share a sidewalk.' The walk completes.

arc.description: Grounded anchor -> evidence pressure -> speculative peak -> grounded return. Stephenson pattern: earn the reach by building concrete pressure first.
